gaps: No existing test case covers the Home Screen UI after pairing a Free20 headset and performing a fresh install of the production app.
No test case verifies that the headset image is displayed on the Home Screen after pairing and fresh install.
The existing test cases focus on onboarding flows (splash screen, EULA, location permissions) but do not cover post-onboarding Home Screen behavior with a paired device.
Missing scenario: Verify that after pairing a Free20 headset and performing a fresh install, the headset image appears correctly on the Home Screen.

generated_testcases.title: Verify headset image appears on Home Screen after pairing Free20 and fresh install of production app
steps: 1. Ensure no previous app data exists (fresh device or clear app data).
2. Install the production version of the app.
3. Launch the app and complete the onboarding flow (splash screen, EULA acceptance, location permission grant).
4. Pair a Free20 headset via Bluetooth following the standard pairing procedure.
5. Once pairing is successful, navigate to the Home Screen.
6. Observe the Home Screen UI for the presence of a headset image/icon.
generated_testcases.expected: The Home Screen displays a headset image/icon representing the paired Free20 device. No placeholder, missing image icon, or blank space is shown where the headset image should be.

generated_testcases.title: Verify headset image persists on Home Screen after app restart with paired Free20
steps: 1. Complete the fresh install and pairing steps from the previous test case.
2. Verify the headset image is present on the Home Screen.
3. Fully close (force stop) the app.
4. Re-launch the app.
5. Observe the Home Screen UI.
generated_testcases.expected: After re-launching the app, the Home Screen still displays the Free20 headset image correctly. The image is not missing or replaced by a broken icon.

generated_testcases.title: Verify headset image is absent on Home Screen before pairing Free20 after fresh install
steps: 1. Ensure no previous app data exists (fresh device or clear app data).
2. Install the production version of the app.
3. Launch the app and complete the onboarding flow (splash screen, EULA acceptance, location permission grant).
4. Do NOT pair any headset.
5. Observe the Home Screen UI.
generated_testcases.expected: The Home Screen does NOT display any headset image/icon, confirming that the headset image only appears after a device is paired.

generated_testcases.title: Verify headset image appears on Home Screen after pairing Free20 and upgrading from previous app version
steps: 1. Install a previous version of the app (e.g., version N-1).
2. Launch the app, complete onboarding, and pair a Free20 headset.
3. Verify the headset image appears on the Home Screen.
4. Upgrade the app to the latest production version (fresh install equivalent via update).
5. Launch the updated app.
6. Observe the Home Screen UI.
generated_testcases.expected: After upgrading to the latest production version, the Home Screen still displays the Free20 headset image correctly. The image is not missing after the update.
